pairs.note: merged = scraped.copy() — result seeded from scraped, confirmed.
`if period not in merged.index: continue` — audited-only periods skipped, never appended. Confirmed by reading the whole reconcile loop (only .loc assignments to existing periods).
Docstring states the deep-backfill contract that reconcile then violates.
Comment claims FSDS backfills deep history — not delivered for scraped symbols.
re.findall(r'\d{6,8}') over the real filename yields [number, date]; verified empirically.
return digits[-1].zfill(8) picks the trailing date; empirically returns 20230331 not 08094273.
number not in wanted -> every real filing skipped -> zero rows.
617 LOC / nesting 14 in analysis.json; module docstring confirms all cycles extracted here.
run_esef_cycle is one of 9 run_* cycles in the same module.
meta_path.write_text is a plain, non-atomic write (data file uses temp-then-rename).
corrupt meta -> {} -> stored ETag lost -> unconditional re-download.
The exact line for audited_frames=None is :199; :200 is price_quote. The supporting statement (`audited_frames=audited if scraped else None`) is in the cited digest window (198-202).
audited_fields column is built from the reconcile result; empty when reconcile didn't run.
`not base.isdigit()` returns None for alphanumeric TSE codes like 130A.
streams iter_bytes to disk with no byte ceiling.
httpx timeout=120 is per-operation, not a total-transfer deadline.
dirty set is purely _newest_raw_stamp > base_mtime.
_newest_raw_stamp globs only data/raw/provider=*/symbol=*.
quote from _price_quotes (prices-latest) is baked into the cached per-symbol row.
dump is written to prices-latest.parquet, outside data/raw — confirmed no write_raw_statement in price_import.
run_refresh fetches GLEIF only when load_mapping()[0] is None.
weekly run_loop timer has the same is-None gate — never refreshes an existing file.
docstring promises 'refreshed at most weekly'; fetch_if_stale max_age is dead under the is-None gate.
num.txt row loop applies no coreg filter (coreg present in test header).
only qtrs/uom/ddate guards; coreg never checked.
equal-rank first-writer-wins per (period,column) — order-dependent when coreg rows collide.
path.read_bytes() loads the whole zip in RAM.
archive.read(inner) decompresses the whole CSV in RAM.
archive.read(...).decode(...) on sub.txt/num.txt materializes whole members.
FRANKFURTER_URL uses /latest — a single spot rate.
one rate map applied across every row/period via zip(field, currency).
run_edinet processes every swept doc by secCode with no docTypeCode filter.
balance branch accepts any present instant; no fiscal-year-end month-day check.
context parse collects start/end/instant only; scenario/dimension members ignored.
fact loop keys by (period,column) with first-seen precedence; consolidation basis undetermined.
newest = max(...); a deletion can only lower it.
dirty requires stamp > base_mtime, so a lowered newest stamp is never dirty.
Code fact confirmed: decode('utf-8'), not utf-8-sig. The harmful outcome is conditional on the live GLEIF file carrying a BOM (unverified).
lower.get('lei') would miss a '\ufeffLEI' key; conditional on a BOM being present.
